name: Nsc (Naca Counseling Subsidiary) - Augusta, Ga
description_short: NACA is a national non-profit housing counseling organization offering character-based mortgage lending with no down payment, closing costs, or credit score requirements to help close the racial wealth gap.
description_long:
NACA (Neighborhood Assistance Corporation of America) was founded in 1988 by the Boston Hotel Workers Union and has spent 36 years as a national advocacy and service organization dedicated to challenging predatory lending practices and expanding affordable homeownership. The organization has served 3 million people, helped 500,000 become homeowners, and originated 75,000 mortgages with $20 billion in mortgage commitments. NACA also provides approximately 30% of all HUD housing counseling services nationally.

NACA offers character-based mortgage lending that explicitly does not consider credit scores, requiring no down payment, no closing costs or fees, and no mortgage insurance. Their mortgages feature below-market fixed rates (as of April 2026: 5.625% for 30-year, 5.125% for 20-year, 5% for 15-year terms). The organization also provides HUD-approved housing counseling, runs "Achieve the Dream" multi-day events in various cities to help people become NACA-qualified borrowers, maintains a member portal for document submission and status tracking, and advocates against predatory landlords and corporate real estate investors.

NACA's core distinction is their explicit mission to close the racial wealth disparity gap through character-based lending that considers borrowers' circumstances rather than credit scores. They combine mortgage origination with robust non-profit advocacy, volunteer opportunities through their "NACtivist" program, and community organizing against predatory lending practices. Their willingness to originate mortgages without down payments or PMI represents a fundamentally different lending model than conventional lenders.

As a non-profit with a 36-year track record and substantial HUD counseling involvement, NACA serves primarily lower-income and historically underserved borrowers. However, prospective borrowers must attend one of their regional "Achieve the Dream" events to qualify and access services, which requires travel and time commitment. The organization's mortgage product is their primary offering, though it functions within their broader advocacy and counseling mission rather than as a traditional consumer finance product.
pros: No credit score consideration—character-based lending focuses on ability to repay rather than credit history
No down payment required—removes largest barrier to homeownership for low-income borrowers
No closing costs or mortgage insurance—eliminates thousands in upfront and annual fees
Below-market fixed rates locked in—5.625% 30-year, 5.125% 20-year, 5% 15-year as of April 2026
HUD-approved housing counseling—provides 30% of all HUD counseling services nationally
36-year track record as established non-profit—500,000+ homeowners helped with $20B in mortgage commitments
Multi-city 'Achieve the Dream' events—in-person support to help borrowers become NACA-qualified
cons: Must attend in-person regional event to qualify—events are multi-day (Friday-Sunday) requiring travel and time commitment
Geographically limited accessibility—only serves specific cities for 'Achieve the Dream' events; no clear indication of coverage outside event locations
Limited information on underwriting standards—website does not explain specific income, employment, or other qualification criteria beyond 'character-based'
No online application or remote qualification process mentioned—all qualification appears to require in-person event attendance
Limited service details—website focuses on mission and mortgages but provides minimal information on loan terms, property restrictions, or approval timelines
best_for: Low-to-moderate income borrowers with poor or no credit history seeking first-time homeownership
Communities disproportionately affected by predatory lending and racial wealth disparities
Borrowers unable to save for down payments or afford closing costs through conventional lenders
First-time homebuyers willing to invest time in counseling and community engagement
diagnosis: NACA is best for low-income and first-time homebuyers with poor credit or no credit history who can attend a regional qualifying event and are seeking mortgages without down payments or closing costs. The primary caveat is that accessing NACA's services requires in-person attendance at a multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' event in select cities—there is no indication of remote qualification, making accessibility dependent on geography and schedule flexibility.
services: Character-based mortgage origination (no credit score required)
Mortgage products with no down payment requirement
Mortgages with no closing costs or fees
Multi-day 'Achieve the Dream' in-person qualification events
Member portal for document submission and status tracking
Advocacy and campaigns against predatory landlords
Partner network access (lenders, servicers, investors, contractors, real estate agents)
typical_results_timeline: Counseling available within 1-2 weeks of contact.
